structures.name: W-Plot / W-Method
structures.description: A double reversal pattern: establish normality, drop into complication, rally toward hope, then crash again before the final climb. The W shape helps balance setbacks and forward motion in commercial pacing.
structures.author: Popularized for novelists by Mary Carroll Moore; related ideas appear in many screenwriting beat models.

phases.title: Status Quo
phases.description: Establish the baseline world, relationships, and what passes for normal.

phases.title: First Downturn
phases.description: An early setback that introduces the problem and lowers the protagonist’s position.

phases.title: First Recovery
phases.description: A rally—hope, progress, or a partial win that still leaves the core wound open.

phases.title: Second Downturn
phases.description: A deeper fall: stakes rise and the old strategy stops working.

phases.title: Second Recovery
phases.description: Another upswing—often a false victory or fragile alliance before the final test.

phases.title: Lowest Point
phases.description: Rock bottom: loss, betrayal, or failure that demands a new kind of courage.

phases.title: Final Rise
phases.description: The climb toward climax using lessons earned through the W-shaped trials.

phases.title: Resolution
phases.description: Show the new equilibrium and how the journey changed the protagonist’s world.
